nodes.name: BUILD_PROMPT.md
observations: Markdown document at repo root (237 lines, 11 sections)
Build prompt for SignTutor v1 — instructs coding agents to ship a production-quality web app from prototype artifacts
Key sections: Role & Objective, Tech Stack, Hard Requirements (9 items), Pages & Routes, Architectural Decisions (7 items), Acceptance Criteria, Definition of Done, Stretch Goals
Prescribes Next.js 14+ App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + onnxruntime-web + MediaPipe Hands

Defines 9 non-negotiable hard requirements: on-device inference, no-account-first-lesson, WCAG 2.2 AA, mirror default, left-handed mode, confidence-gated verdict, per-finger panel, JS↔Python parity, CI release gates
Documents 7 architectural decisions: hybrid ML+rule, auto-fallback to rule, collision-aware verdict, first-frame trajectory anchor, rotation-aug training, on-device lighting check, latency HUD
Lists 6 v1 pages/routes: /, /setup, /learn/asl/fingerspelling/:letter, /learn/asl/words/:slug, /practice, /about, /privacy
References prototype/, ml/, ci/, pdr-review/, and .github/workflows/ as existing assets to consume
Tests with Vitest + Playwright; deploys to Vercel

nodes.name: BUILD_PROMPT_ML_EXTENSION.md
observations: Markdown document at repo root (437 lines, 18 sections)

Build prompt for SignTutor v2 ML Extension — replaces synthetic data with real data, adds motion letters, NMM scoring, personalization, confidence calibration, multi-language, continuous-sign research preview

8 workstreams: W1 Real data, W2 J/Z motion letters, W3 Vocabulary scale-out (8→200), W4 NMM scoring, W5 On-device personalization, W6 Confidence calibration, W7 Multi-language, W8 Continuous-sign research preview
Assumes v1 is shipped and functional in signtutor/ directory
Preserves all v1 user-facing invariants while substantially expanding ML capabilities
References signtutor/ (v1 shipped site), ml/ (data gen + training), .github/workflows/ (CI gates)
New hard requirements include: signer-disjoint train/test splits, privacy-preserving personalization, confidence calibration curves, i18n curriculum bundles

nodes.name: README.md
observations: Markdown document at repo root (79 lines, 8 sections)
Project README for SignTutor — browser-based, vision-driven sign language learning platform with on-device inference
Quick start: cd signtutor, npm install, npm run dev
8 available commands: dev, build, start, lint, typecheck, test, test:watch, test:e2e
Testing: Vitest unit tests in __tests__/, Playwright E2E in e2e/
Project structure: src/app/, src/lib/, src/components/, public/models/, __tests__/, e2e/, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
Privacy emphasis: on-device by default, no account required, no analytics during practice
MIT licensed

nodes.name: package.json

Package name: signlanguage, version 1.0.0
Repository: github.com/afilfarhan/signlanguage
License: ISC
Only dependency: playwright ^1.60.0
Test script is placeholder (echo only, no test framework configured at root level)
Root-level package.json — actual app lives under signtutor/

nodes.name: requirements.txt
observations: Text file at repo root (28 lines)
Python dependencies for the ML pipeline in ml/
Key packages: mediapipe, onnxruntime, numpy, scikit-learn, torch, torchvision, pillow, opencv-python, matplotlib, pandas, seaborn, jupyter
Supports the Python-side data generation, training, and evaluation scripts

nodes.name: signtutor-redesign-prompt.md
observations: Markdown document at repo root (430 lines, 27 sections)
Complete redesign build prompt for SignTutor with 'Bioluminescent Studio' aesthetic direction
Dark, focused visual theme: near-black backgrounds, bioluminescent teal accent (#00e5b0), electric blue (#4f8cff), amber (#ffb347)
Design tokens defined for backgrounds, brand colors, typography, spacing
10 pages specified: Home, About, Learn, Fingerspelling, Letter Practice (×2), Settings, Words, Practice, Setup Wizard
Global UI components: Navbar, Cards, Category Badges, Buttons
Animation & motion system including page transitions, micro-interactions, reduced-motion support
Responsive breakpoints and WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility requirements
Preserves all functional ML/inference logic — UI/UX layer redesign only
Stack: Next.js + Tailwind CSS (existing, no stack change)
